title: Oxford Aging Cell paper: rapamycin rejuvenates the immune system by protecting T cell DNA
description: A new Oxford study in Aging Cell reports that rapamycin reduces DNA double-strand breaks in T cells, doubles their survival after damage, and decreases senescence markers in older adults — adding a mechanism (immune-cell DNA protection) to mTOR's well-known anti-aging activity. Combined with PEARL's first long-term human RCT data, this is now the strongest mechanistic + clinical stack for rapamycin as a geroprotector.

title: University of Sydney: 4-week dietary intervention reduces biological age in adults 65-75
description: A 104-person University of Sydney RCT published in Aging Cell tested four diets in adults aged 65-75 for 4 weeks; all groups except high-fat omnivore showed reductions in biological age, with the biggest improvement in the higher-carb omnivore arm. This is one of the cleanest short-window demonstrations that diet changes can shift longevity-relevant biomarkers in older adults.

title: GLP-1 semaglutide aging story dominates global longevity coverage
description: UC San Diego's randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial showing semaglutide slowed DunedinPACE biological aging by 9% in 108 HIV patients spread rapidly through SciTechDaily, Yahoo Health, and the popular press on June 3. With a related npj Aging pilot showing 42% slower aging and 49% telomere lengthening, GLP-1s are now the most-discussed pharmacological geroprotector of the week.

title: Biotech ETFs rebound sharply: IBB +1.97%, XBI +1.62%, BBH +2.22%
description: After two consecutive red sessions, longevity-adjacent biotech ETFs bounced back Wednesday: IBB +1.97% ($7.68B AUM), XBI +1.62% ($8.43B), BBH +2.22%, SBIO +1.41%, XLV +0.79%. The 2026 biotech M&A tape — $106B across 201 deals YTD (CNBC) — and NewLimit's $435M Series C are likely supporting the recovery.

title: Sinclair-Tally Health evidence map names winning epigenetic-aging interventions
description: The 41-study Sinclair / Adiv Johnson evidence map (Frontiers in Genetics) is being widely re-covered today, naming exercise, semaglutide, pitavastatin, plant-rich diets, caloric restriction, omega-3s and multivitamins as interventions with positive next-generation epigenetic clock evidence — and flagging many widely promoted longevity protocols as unproven.

title: Oxford: rapamycin protects T cells from DNA damage and immune-system aging
description: Dr. Kell and colleagues report in Aging Cell that mTOR inhibition with rapamycin reduces DNA double-strand breaks in T cells, doubles T-cell survival after damage, and lowers senescence markers in older adults supplementing low-dose rapamycin.

title: University of Sydney: short-term dietary patterns shift biological age in older adults
description: A 104-person, 4-week RCT in Aging Cell from the University of Sydney shows that three of four dietary patterns reduced biological-age scores in adults aged 65-75; the higher-carb omnivore arm produced the largest improvement.

title: SciTechDaily: popular weight-loss drug slows biological aging in landmark trial
description: SciTechDaily covers the UC San Diego semaglutide RCT showing slower epigenetic aging across multiple clocks; DunedinPACE pace of aging slowed 9% and PCGrimAge mortality risk markers significantly reduced.

title: UCSD: GLP-1 semaglutide may slow biological aging across organ systems
description: UCSD researchers detail the Nature Communications RCT in 108 adults with HIV-associated lipohypertrophy: semaglutide slowed aging across inflammation, brain, heart, kidney, liver, blood and metabolic clocks, including a 9% DunedinPACE slowdown.

title: NewLimit raises $435M Series C — biggest pure longevity round, Phase 1 in 2027
description: South San Francisco's NewLimit, co-founded by Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong, raised $435M in Series C to launch a Phase 1 trial of its first epigenetic reprogramming therapy for alcohol-related liver disease in 2027.

title: Fierce Biotech 2026 fundraising tracker: NewLimit $435M leads year
description: Fierce Biotech's 2026 fundraising tracker logs NewLimit's $435M Series C as one of the largest private biotech rounds of the year, alongside continued capital flow into aging and reprogramming startups.

title: CNBC: 2026 biotech M&A hits $106B across 201 transactions year-to-date
description: CNBC reports biotech deals reached $106B across 201 transactions YTD 2026, signaling a resurgent M&A environment likely supporting the biotech-ETF rebound.

title: Sinclair + Adiv Johnson publish first comprehensive evidence map of human epigenetic aging interventions
description: The Frontiers in Genetics review analyzes 41 human studies and identifies which interventions have evidence on next-generation epigenetic aging clocks, including exercise, semaglutide, pitavastatin, plant-rich diets, caloric restriction, omega-3s and multivitamins.

title: Yahoo Finance amplifies Sinclair-Johnson epigenetic aging review
description: Yahoo Finance amplifies the Sinclair-Johnson evidence map, framing it as the most extensive synthesis of human studies on next-generation epigenetic aging clocks to date.

title: Cyclarity UDP-003 Phase 1: first-in-human oxidized cholesterol excretion data
description: Cyclarity Therapeutics presented Phase 1 data at AHA Vascular Discovery showing UDP-003 — a cyclodextrin targeting toxic 7-ketocholesterol — produced measurable oxidized cholesterol excretion, the first clinical proof of concept for clearing soft-plaque oxidized cholesterol.

title: Lifespan Rejuvenation Roundup May 2026: senescence, mitochondria, time-restricted feeding
description: Lifespan.io's monthly digest covers primary vs secondary senescence, phosphatidylcholine decline in mitochondrial aging, time-restricted feeding restoring stem cell function, and methylene blue protection of hair follicle stem cells.

title: UT Health San Antonio launches multi-phase rapamycin healthspan study
description: Dr. David Kellogg's team at UT Health San Antonio is enrolling non-diabetic 65-90 year-olds in a six-week rapamycin or everolimus trial with a four-month follow-up to map vascular, metabolic and functional endpoints.

title: NMN: PEARL — first long-term human rapamycin RCT shows muscle and bone benefits
description: The PEARL trial reports no serious adverse events over 48 weeks of weekly rapamycin in older adults; 10 mg/week preserved muscle in women and bone mineral content in men.

title: Senescence News June 3 digest: vgll3 gene, sleep and aging, GLP-1 knee replacement
description: This week's Senescence News digest highlights vgll3 as a gene that promotes rapid growth and early reproduction while increasing cancer and aging risk later, sleep problems in mid-life women predicting psychological well-being a decade later, and GLP-1 receptor agonists being linked to significantly lower long-term knee-replacement risk.

title: Sutter Health: Dr. Steve Cummings on longevity in older adults
description: Sutter Health profiles Dr. Steve Cummings, a longtime healthspan researcher whose work on osteoporosis, falls and frailty informs current longevity medicine practice.

title: Peter Attia Drive #394: sleep pharmacology and Alzheimer's prevention
description: Peter Attia's new episode covers sleep pharmacology, common prescription sleep medications and their effects on sleep architecture, and the promise of dual orexin receptor antagonists (DORAs) for Alzheimer's prevention.

title: Bryan Johnson Blueprint peptide list updated for 2026
description: Updated peptide stack covers Bryan Johnson's Blueprint Peptide Serum (eight biomimetic peptides applied to the scalp), 20-30 g/day collagen peptides with vitamin C, and an Epithalon/Thymulin 10 mg IM x 5 days every six months protocol.

title: NewLimit aging reprogramming raise rolls through Instagram, Facebook longevity press
description: Instagram and social channels propagate the NewLimit $435M Series C story: 'reversing cellular aging' framing and plans to begin human studies next year for the first drug targeting alcohol-related liver disease.

title: The Vitals HealthSpan miniseries debuts (episode 1)
description: The Vitals launched the first episode of a six-part HealthSpan miniseries, signaling continued mainstream consumer-media interest in longevity protocols and clinics.

title: Engineering Post primer: senolytics designed to clear zombie cells
description: A mainstream Engineering Post primer summarizes the senolytic class, including senescent cell clearance and emerging evidence on tissue health, muscle function and biological-age markers — with notes on long-term safety still pending.

title: News Medical: mammals share universal gene signatures as they age
description: New cross-species research reveals universal gene signatures that mammals share as they age, opening new candidate 'molecular clocks' that could be targeted to extend healthy lifespan across species.

title: Peter Attia: resistance training — lowering the barrier to entry
description: Attia synthesizes new evidence on minimum-effective resistance-training protocols (LM2: low-load, multiple sets, twice weekly) showing meaningful strength, hypertrophy and mobility benefits in untrained older adults.

title: Washington Post: small changes that can meaningfully improve health and longevity
description: Washington Post longevity feature surveys the literature on small, sustained lifestyle shifts (sleep, walking, resistance training, plant-rich meals) that meaningfully improve health and longevity in middle-aged and older adults.

title: Bryan Johnson floats biological-age study of crypto industry workers
description: Bryan Johnson publicly proposed measuring biological age in cryptocurrency industry workers to test whether the high-stress, irregular-sleep sector accelerates aging — likely a follow-on Blueprint marketing campaign.

title: Fight Aging June 1 newsletter: ProAL50 allostatic-load index and multi-omics clocks
description: This week's Fight Aging digest leads with the ProAL50 proteomic allostatic-load index, multi-omics clock convergence, nanomaterials in osteoarthritis cartilage and the genetics of exceptional longevity.
